mappings.rationale: Lowrie covers the Earth's concentric layers, seismic wave analysis, and the geophysical evidence used to infer the structure and composition of the Earth's interior.
Redfern provides a comprehensive treatment of the Earth's internal structure including the crust, mantle, core, and the Mohorovicic discontinuity.
Molnar discusses the structure of the crust and mantle, the distinction between continental and oceanic crust, and the dynamics of the Earth's interior that drive plate motions.
Zalasiewicz covers the layered structure of the Earth, earthquake processes, and the geochemical differentiation of the Earth's interior.
Vaughan discusses the mineral composition of the Earth's layers, relevant to the distribution of elements in the core, mantle, and crust.
Covers the composition of rocks forming the Earth's crust and the processes that produce them, directly relevant to understanding crustal and mantle composition.
McGuire covers earthquakes—their causes, distribution, magnitude, and seismic measurement—central topics in this section's treatment of seismic sources.
Volcanism provides direct evidence of the Earth's interior composition and the molten state of the outer core and mantle processes.
Lenton discusses the development of the Earth's structure and composition as part of the integrated Earth system.
